name: Spinach Spuds
recipeIngredient: 4 cups unpeeled and diced white potatoes
1 teaspoon bottled hot sauce (optional), up to 2
recipeInstructions:
Makes 4 servings
I have fond memories of my father taking me to a local diner on Saturdays
for breakfast when I was young. I had a ravenous appetite and frequently
cleaned off the home fries from his plate as well as mine. To this day I
still love potatoes in the morning, but without the bacon drippings and
fat. The fresh spinach used here is a healthful alternative.
1. Place the potatoes in enough boiling water to cover and cook for about 15 minutes,
until tender. Drain in a colander and cool slightly.
2. Heat the oil in a medium skillet over low heat. Add the onion and garlic and sauté for
about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Stir in the potatoes, spinach, scallions, paprika, salt, cayenne pepper, and bottled hot sauce,
if desired. Cook for 5 to 7 minutes, stirring frequently until all ingredients are heated through.
4. Serve hot with ketchup on the side.
